load_bearing_defect: D1 (Indusind cheque-by-remark width ^[0-9]{5,6}$ -> ^[0-9]{5}$): a rare-trigger domain rule that fires only when the remark field is a bare 5-6 digit instrument (cheque) number; ordinary alphanumeric/empty remarks never feed it. Its correct upper bound 6 is pinned by SEVEN identical repo-wide occurrences of ^[0-9]{5,6}$ (Axis, BankOfMaharashtra, PNB, Indusind). This is the analogue of the sibling's ^[0-9]{3,5}$ cheque-remark rule (task 2) and the 16-char zero-prefixed cheque-remark rule (task 1) that failed Opus.
litmus_result: PASS: leak check (git log --oneline | wc -l == 1, git status clean, git diff empty, all 5 planted markers present in built image, zero correct forms remaining). NULL run (--network none) reward 0 with all 5 f2p reporting per-test FAILED (no collection errors) and 14/14 p2p passing. ORACLE run (solve.sh) reward 1 with 19/19 required passing. PARTIAL run (revert only D1+D4) reward 0 with the other 3 f2p (D2,D3,D5) still FAILED. FAIRNESS run (materially different correct fixes: ^[0-9]{5}[0-9]?$ width, amount.isin([1]) sentinel, accountType!="CURRENT" gate, sep_by_dash(3,5) final-segment slice, index=0 restoration) reward 1 / 19 pass.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (the repo ships no test files; the agent-visible tree has nothing to run and no failing local test points at any defect). The gold boundary suite enters only at grade time via config.json test_patch; against the clean repo it is 19/19 green, at planted exactly the 5 f2p fail and all 14 p2p stay green.
env_note: No task-specific env, secrets, or network required. The per-bank categorization is deterministic and offline. Repo image (txenrich-repo:v1) is python:3.11-slim with numpy==1.26.4 / pandas==2.2.3 installed as wheels; the package is installed editable with --no-deps so the 2022-era setup.py pins (which have no cp311/arm64 wheels) are bypassed. The np.select string/amount logic runs unchanged on the modern pins; verified by exercising both bank categorizers directly (probe_base / probe_defected).

defects.edge: a DEBIT whose remark is a bare 6-digit instrument (cheque) number (the upper edge of the accepted 5-6 digit width)
defects.derivability: The accepted width is pinned by SEVEN identical repo-wide occurrences of ^[0-9]{5,6}$ (Axis lines 58-59, BankOfMaharashtra 73-74, PNB 60-61, Indusind 64), all keyed to the same 5-6 digit instrument-number width; the singleton ^[0-9]{5}$ contradicts them and a grep of the remark idiom reveals the anomaly. Nothing in any test signposts it.
defects.wrong_output: the bare 6-digit-remark debit is no longer labelled CHEQUE_PAID (falls through to the default TRANSFER)

defects.edge: a rupee-one mandate-registration credit (description contains MANDATE, amount exactly 1)
defects.derivability: A sentinel-amount slip pinned by the pervasive amount.eq(1) idiom used 11 times across the repo to mark rupee-one verification / penny-drop credits, and by the adjacent amount.isin(["1","-1"]) / amount.isin(["1"]) verification siblings on the lines directly around it. eq(2) matches no verification transaction; a grep of the sentinel idiom reveals the singleton.
defects.wrong_output: the rupee-one mandate credit is no longer labelled ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION (falls through to TRANSFER)

defects.edge: a POS TXN credit >= 10 in a SAVING account (card-payment reversal path)
defects.derivability: A casing/spelling typo pinned by the repo-wide accountType == "SAVING" idiom (6 occurrences), the adjacent "CURRENT" convention (4 occurrences), and directly by the very next rule (line 27, same POS-TXN-credit-savings path) which keeps "SAVING". The caller never passes "SAVINGS", so the branch never fires; a grep of the account-type literal reveals the singleton.
defects.wrong_output: the savings-account POS reversal credit falls through to the line-27 REFUND rule instead of CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL

defects.edge: an ACH mandate CREDIT whose payee name is the final dash-segment of an ACH-BD-NACH-<NAME> layout
defects.derivability: A fixed-layout slice index pinned by the guard on the SAME line: the literal ^ACH-BD-NACH has three dash-delimited prefix tokens (ACH, BD, NACH) before the payee, so the payee is segment index 3. The adjacent sibling ^ACH-BD- -> sep_by_dash(...,2,3) encodes the same layout->index mapping (two prefix tokens -> index 2), as does ^ACH- -> (1,2). Shifting to segment 2 grabs the literal 'NACH' instead of the name.
defects.wrong_output: the payee name comes out as 'Nach' (a fixed layout token) instead of the actual employer name

defects.edge: a ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{10,} <NAME> structured transfer whose payee is the single capture group of its layout
defects.derivability: A capture-group index slip pinned by the same-line pattern: [0-9]{10,} (.*) has exactly one capture group, so the only valid extract index is 0; index=1 references a non-existent group and py_extract returns empty (its .get(1) is None -> pd.Series('')). Pinned further by the pervasive index=0 idiom on single-group patterns across both scripts (IDBI 3x, Indusind 70x, zero index=1 anywhere). Requires understanding py_extract's .get(index) semantics and counting the pattern's groups.
defects.wrong_output: the transfer payee is no longer extracted (partyName stays empty)

resistance_assessment: Five single-token slips across two of ~40 bank scripts (IDBI, Indusind), each biting only an exact-width / sentinel-amount / account-type / fixed-layout edge that ordinary transactions never feed, so there is no failing local test and no run-pytest-see-red gradient (the repo ships no tests at all). A solve requires finding ALL five, which are deliberately five distinct shapes (a regex quantifier upper bound, an integer sentinel literal, a string-casing typo, a dash-slice segment index, a capture-group index) so no single pattern-match surfaces the rest. Two are easy-derivable (the amount.eq(1) sentinel greppable against 11 correct siblings, and the SAVING casing against 6 plus the adjacent line-27 twin; p~0.9); three are medium (the ^[0-9]{5,6}$ width pinned by counting/greping the seven sibling occurrences, the ACH-BD-NACH slice pinned by the same-line dash-count layout plus the adjacent siblings, the capture-group index pinned by the single-group pattern plus the index=0 idiom). D1 is the load-bearing rare-trigger: it fires only when the remark is a bare 5-6 digit instrument number, exactly the untested edge, the direct analogue of the sibling cheque-remark rules that failed Opus. Targeting Opus ~0-4/10.
fairness_audit: Every gold test is pure-input: it builds a real pandas DataFrame of raw transaction fields and calls the actual categorize_IDBI_transactions / categorize_Indusind_transactions, asserting on the returned category / payee columns. There are ZERO mocks, ZERO patched attributes, and no reliance on any private helper name; a materially different but correct fix still passes (verified end-to-end to reward 1: ^[0-9]{5}[0-9]?$ for the width, amount.isin([1]) for the sentinel, accountType!="CURRENT" for the account gate, sep_by_dash(3,5) for the final-segment slice, and index-0 restoration). Each f2p is disjoint (different bank/branch/input) and each has a p2p that pins the adjacent correct behavior at both base and planted state (the 5-digit and 7-digit remarks for D1; the ordinary +5000 mandate and the neutral rupee-one for D2; the below-ten savings REFUND and the CURRENT-account gate for D3; the sibling ACH-BD slice and the AUTO_CREDIT category for D4; the short-reference guard-miss and the ATM_WITHDRAWAL category for D5). All five defected branches are reachable from the live categorizers (verified by direct exercise). Deterministic and fully offline.
